queue.question: Sanity check: test 3-5 known historical events with well-documented outcomes (e.g., Lehman 2008-09-15 -> XLF crash, TSLA S&P 500 addition 2020-12-21 -> run-up, COVID crash 2020-03-12 -> broad market) using measure_event_impact(). Verify that our measurements produce the expected direction and approximate magnitude. This calibrates trust in the data pipeline before doing real research.
queue.reasoning: Before investing sessions in real research, verify the measurement pipeline produces sensible results. If Lehman doesn't show a negative abnormal return for financials, nothing else we measure can be trusted.
queue.findings:
Pipeline calibration PASSED. Tested 3 known historical events:
1. META Q4 2022 (Feb 1, 2023): measured +21.82% abnormal 1d return (expected ~+23% raw). MATCH.
2. GOOGL Q3 2022 (Oct 25, 2022): measured -8.38% abnormal 1d return (expected ~-9% raw). MATCH.
3. NVDA Q4 FY2024 (Feb 21, 2024): measured +14.33% abnormal 1d return (expected ~+16% raw). MATCH.
Benchmark subtraction working correctly (abnormal < raw as expected). Timing='after_hours' correctly uses prior close as reference price. Pipeline is ready for systematic research.

queue.question: What is the magnitude and duration of post-earnings announcement drift (PEAD) for S&P 500 stocks that beat consensus EPS by >10%? Is the effect still tradeable after decades of being published?
queue.reasoning: PEAD is the most robustly documented anomaly in academic finance (Ball & Brown 1968, Bernard & Thomas 1989). Starting here gives us a well-studied baseline to calibrate our methodology against known results before exploring less-charted territory.
queue.findings: PEAD does NOT hold for mega-cap S&P 500 companies with >10% EPS beats. Discovery (2021-2022, n=20 events): avg abnormal 1d = +1.05%, positive_rate = 45%, 1d->5d drift = -0.94%. Not statistically significant. Financials (2020-2021, n=13): avg abnormal 1d = +0.40%, 1d->5d drift = -1.50%. DEAD END for mega-caps. Exception: 2023-2024 tech shows +8.37% avg abnormal but confounded by AI/META regime. Potential follow-up: test PEAD on smaller S&P 500 components (market cap < $50B).

queue.question: Backtest small biotech (market cap <$5B at time of announcement) PDUFA decisions 2018-2024: measure abnormal returns at 1d, 5d, 10d after CRL rejection vs approval. CRITICAL: include delisted companies (MUST search for failed biotech companies that received CRLs). Test: does CRL deficiency type (CMC vs clinical) predict magnitude of decline? Minimum 20 events per category (approval vs rejection). Potential hypothesis: SHORT small biotech after CRL, 10-day hold.
queue.reasoning: Literature confirms large, asymmetric, persistent effect for small biotech on CRL/rejection. Survivorship bias risk is high - must search for delisted companies explicitly. This could be a more reliable pattern than large-cap FDA decisions. Post-rejection drift makes this a potential trading signal.
queue.findings:
Partial: ran FDA CRL backtest (N=8 measured, 4 delisted due to survivorship bias). 
CRITICAL FINDING: CMC-only CRLs (AXSM, SRRK) show POSITIVE 1d returns. Clinical CRLs show high variance: OTLK -81%, APLT -77% vs ITRM +18%, NERV +1.2%.
Dead end recorded for GENERIC CRL short. Cannot form hypothesis due to: 33% data quality failure, stdev=41%, critical CMC vs clinical distinction.
NEXT STEP: (1) Need alt data for SESN/ATNX/ICPT (SEC filings?). (2) Focus on clinical CRL + single-asset company sub-hypothesis. (3) Add data quality task for delisted biotech tickers.

queue.question: Backtest the insider buying cluster hypothesis: when 2+ corporate insiders (officers/directors) make open-market stock purchases within 30 days of each other (each >$50K), does the stock outperform over the next 1 month and 3 months? Use SEC EDGAR quarterly bulk data (free, no API key). Cover 2018-2024. Filter for market cap >$500M to ensure tradeable. Academic benchmark: Kang, Kim & Wang (2018) found 3.8% abnormal returns over 21 days for cluster purchases vs 2.0% for non-cluster. Key risk: signal may have decayed in liquid stocks post-2018.
queue.reasoning: Feasibility confirmed: SEC provides free quarterly bulk ZIPs. Data contains NONDERIV_TRANS.tsv (transaction code P = open market purchase, shares, price), SUBMISSION.tsv (ticker), REPORTINGOWNER.tsv (director/officer flag). End-to-end test on Q4 2024 found 1,330 qualified purchases and 487 cluster events in one quarter alone. Literature supports the signal (2001-2018 data). The 2020-2024 signal strength is the open empirical question. Build tools/insider_cluster_detector.py first, then pre-register hypothesis before measuring returns.
queue.findings: Tool built at tools/insider_cluster_detector.py. Discovery backtest (2020-2021) and OOS (2022) both validated. See knowledge_base.json insider_buying_cluster entry.
queue.implementation_notes: 1. Download ZIPs: https://www.sec.gov/files/structureddata/data/insider-transactions-data-sets/{YEAR}q{Q}_form345.zip for 2018Q1 through 2024Q4. 2. Parse NONDERIV_TRANS (TRANS_CODE=P, TRANS_SHARES*TRANS_PRICEPERSHARE>50000). 3. Join with SUBMISSION (ticker) and REPORTINGOWNER (Director or Officer roles). 4. Group by ticker+30-day window, find 2+ different insiders. 5. Filter for market cap >$500M at event date (use yfinance). 6. Output: list of cluster event dates + tickers. 7. Run measure_event_impact() on events. 8. Split discovery (2018-2021) vs validation (2022-2024). Min 3 validation instances required.

queue.question: Run full population backtest on 2020-2021 discovery period using all measurable events (not just 200-event samples). Use the 2427-event filtered pool, measure all at once to get true population statistics. Note: will still have 40% drop rate.
queue.reasoning: Current results are from 200-event samples with only 116-126 measured. Full population measurement would give ~1450 measured events for much stronger inference.
queue.findings: COMPLETED in previous session. Full population 2020-2021 (n=1452 measured, 40% failure rate): Signal peaks at 3d (+2.93%, p<0.000001), 5d +2.64% (p<0.000001). passes_multiple_testing=True (4/5 horizons p<0.01). 20d weak (+1.20%, p=0.030, negative median). Discovery confirms: 5d is optimal hold for 2+ insiders. Full population results more reliable than 200-sample.

queue.question: 2022 out-of-sample validation: run the cluster signal on 2022 data (1164 events in our dataset). Discovery was 2020-2021. The 2022 sample includes a bear market regime which differs from 2020-2021. Compare effect sizes across regimes.
queue.reasoning: Mandatory OOS validation per methodology. Need at least 3 validation instances at minimum. 2022 gives a full year in a different market regime.
queue.findings: COMPLETED in previous session. 2022 OOS (n=816 measured): Signal CONFIRMED and STRONGER than discovery. 3d +3.89% (p<0.000001), 5d +3.44% (p<0.000001), 20d +3.67% (p<0.000001). Positive rate 59-69%. passes_multiple_testing=True (5/5). 2022 was a bear market which provides excellent regime robustness test - signal held. Confirms hypothesis 76678219 (2+ insiders, 5d hold) and validates the signal in non-bull regime.

queue.question: Run 2023-2024 OOS validation on 3+ insider cluster hypothesis (4a981cc8). Download 2023Q1-2024Q4 quarterly bulk data, filter for 3+ insider clusters, measure abnormal returns. This is mandatory for hypothesis confidence - currently only have 2021 discovery + 2022 OOS. Need 2023-2024 as third validation period. Also: check if signal decays in post-2022 bull market regime.
queue.reasoning: Per methodology: minimum 3 validation instances. Currently have discovery (2021) + OOS 2022. Need at least one more OOS period before elevating confidence to 9/10.
queue.findings: 2023-24 OOS COMPLETE (n=311): 1d pos_rate=55.3% (WEAKENED from 72-80%), 3d pos_rate=69.1% avg=+4.71% p<0.0001 (STRONG). Signal not decaying at 3d. Best hold=3d. Use 1cb6140f (3d hold) for GO trade.

queue.question: Create new hypothesis 'insider_cluster_3d_hold': 3+ insiders, buy at open day after filing, hold 3 trading days. Evidence: 3d avg +3.80% (2023-24 OOS), +3.89% (2022 OOS), +2.93% (2021 disc). This is a BETTER specification than the 1d hypothesis (4a981cc8) based on accumulated evidence. Pre-register before next trade. Use GO paper trade result to calibrate.
queue.reasoning: 3d signal is more reliable and larger than 1d (p<0.0001 vs p=0.006, avg +3.5-4% vs +1-1.5%). New hypothesis would be better evidence-aligned. Confidence 7/10 expected.
queue.findings: 3d hypothesis already pre-registered as 1cb6140f. 2023-24 OOS confirms 3d optimal: 69.1% pos rate, +4.71% avg, p<0.0001.

queue.question: Do large share buyback announcements (>5% market cap, or first-ever program) generate positive abnormal returns in the 3-5d window? Academic literature suggests +2-4%. Event source: 8-K Item 8.01. Advantage: announced any time, not just at earnings.
queue.reasoning: Buyback signal has strong literature support, clear event timing (8-K or press release), and clean causal mechanism (reduced float + management signal of undervaluation). Need to find events via EDGAR Item 8.01 with share repurchase filter for >5% market cap magnitude.
queue.findings: SIGNAL WEAKENED BY LITERATURE. Key findings: (1) Academic consensus: long-horizon abnormal returns on buyback announcements dropped to near-zero post-2007 as markets became efficient to this signal. (2) Short-term (3d) CAR is still ~1.4-1.6% per European studies, but US large-caps show diminishing effect. (3) First-ever buyback announcements are NOT reliably positive -- Salesforce first-ever B buyback (Aug 2022) caused a 10% DROP because investors read it as management signaling low-growth outlook. (4) Large routine buybacks (Apple B, MSFT B, etc.) have muted 1d stock reactions because they are expected and priced in. (5) The sub-signal with most promise: small/mid-cap companies announcing buyback for first time AND buying back >10% of float. Academic paper (Vermaelen) documents 16% long-horizon excess returns for large tender-offer buybacks, but tender offers are rare and well-known. (6) DECISION: Do not backtest this as-is. The causal mechanism (undervaluation signaling) is well-documented but efficiency has eroded the edge. NEXT STEP: If pursuing, narrow to (a) first-ever programs at companies with <B market cap, (b) buying back >10% of shares outstanding, (c) announced with earnings miss (contradicts growth pessimism narrative). This sub-hypothesis has not been tested and may have edge.

Review these entries at each session start and set triggers if appropriate. First check: did POOL (Pool Corp, 3 insiders, $3.6M, filed March 17) or AMRZ (5 insiders, $4.6M, filed March 16) generate returns? Also look for new clusters that the scanner identifies.

queue.question: MONITOR: S&P 500 Q2 2026 rebalance announcement expected June 5, 2026 (first Friday of June). Action: (1) On or before June 5, run tools/sp500_change_scanner.py --check-now to detect announcement. (2) For each confirmed addition, buy $5,000 LONG at Monday June 8 open. (3) Hold 5 trading days, exit June 15. Evidence: March 2026 additions showed avg 1d=+5.20%, 3d=+7.66%, 100% positive rate (VRT+4.2%, LITE+10.0%, COHR+5.9%, SATS+0.7%).
queue.reasoning: We missed the March 2026 rebalance because it wasn't in our watchlist (only June was). The signal validated again: 100% positive rate at 3d, avg +7.66%. June 2026 is next opportunity. We have $100K cash and max 5 active experiments - plenty of room for this trade.

queue.question: When a stock is announced for S&P 500 addition, what is the abnormal return between announcement and effective date, and does it reverse after inclusion? How does this compare to Russell 2000 rebalancing?
queue.reasoning: Index inclusion has a clear causal mechanism (forced buying by index funds). Well-studied, recent examples are easy to find, and the effect window is well-defined. Good second target for methodology calibration.
queue.findings: S&P 500 index addition effect is REAL and SIGNIFICANT in 2020-2024, contradicting Greenwood & Sammon (2025). Discovery (2020-2022, n=15): 1d abnormal +4.09%, 5d +5.52%, 73% positive. Passes multiple testing. Validation (2023-2024, n=10): 1d abnormal +8.05%, 5d +10.23%, 100% positive. Passes multiple testing. Hypothesis 061ae3a8 created (confidence 9/10, long after announcement, 5d hold, 5% expected abnormal). CAVEATS: 2 missing events (SBNY bankrupt, CTLT acquired). Thematic stocks (TSLA, MRNA, SMCI, PLTR) inflate results but non-thematic additions still show +3-7% 1d effect. NEXT: Add to event watchlist for Q2 2026 rebalance (~June 6 announcement). Also explore: (1) deletion effect (short after removal announced), (2) post-effective-date reversal.

queue.question: Test S&P 500 DELETION effect (when stocks are REMOVED from the S&P 500): measure abnormal returns at 1d, 3d, 5d, 10d after deletion announcement. Hypothesis: stocks removed from S&P 500 show significant NEGATIVE abnormal returns. Literature says deletion effect historically was -12-16% (larger than addition effect). Check if deletion effect is also still present in 2020-2024. Note: many deletions are due to M&A (acquisition at premium) - filter these out.
queue.reasoning: Complement to the S&P 500 addition effect we documented. If deletion effect is real, could SHORT stocks after deletion announcement. Literature says deletion effect was historically larger than addition. Easy to test using same data sources.
queue.findings: Deletion SHORT effect tested (N=21, 2021-2026). 3d avg=-1.99% (p=0.0078) but 1d avg=-0.44% (p=0.59). FAILS OUT-OF-SAMPLE: validation (N=13) 1d avg=+0.27% POSITIVE, 3d p=0.015 does not pass Bonferroni. DEAD END: severe selection bias, contrarian buyers offset index fund selling on D+1.

queue.question: Test S&P 500 addition POST-EFFECTIVE-DATE REVERSAL: after the effective date passes, do added stocks give back their gains? Measure abnormal returns starting from effective date (not announcement date) for horizons 1d, 5d, 10d, 20d post-effective. If stocks revert after effective date (index fund buying complete), this suggests the pre-effective run-up is entirely price pressure and not fundamental. Test on same 25 events from the addition backtest.
queue.reasoning: The classic S&P 500 inclusion literature disagrees on whether the addition effect is permanent or temporary. If temporary (price pressure), stocks should revert post-effective-date. If permanent (investor recognition), they should hold their gains. This will tell us how long to hold the position (sell before effective date vs hold longer).
queue.findings: Post-effective-date reversal NOT present (N=24, 2020-2024). 1d avg=-0.61% (p=0.36), 5d avg=-0.41% (p=0.59), 20d avg=+1.16% (p=0.70). NONE significant. 0 horizons pass MT. Gains are PERMANENT (investor recognition), not temporary price pressure. The 5-day hold is optimal.

queue.question:

Specifically: SESN (Sesen Bio - CRL Aug 13 2021, known ~-80% 1d), ATNX (Athenex - CRL Mar 1 2021, known -21% 1d),

(3) Alternative data APIs (Tiingo, Polygon). If data found, add to CRL backtest and check if clinical-only CRL short
hypothesis becomes statistically significant. Also add 5+ more clinical CRL events from 2019-2024 to build N=15+.
queue.reasoning: FDA CRL backtest needs delisted company data. SESN/ATNX/ICPT are the highest-impact events and their absence understates the true effect. Critical for survivorship bias correction.
queue.findings: Tiingo has no data for SESN/ATNX (known delisted). CORV also unavailable. RETA data found via Tiingo but was pre-anticipated via adcomm vote, not useful. FDA released 200+ CRLs publicly via open.fda.gov API (https://api.fda.gov/transparency/crl.json). New measurable clinical CRLs found: ARDX Jul 19 2021 (-74% crash), APLT Nov 27 2024 (-77%), ALDX Mar 16 2026 (-70%), BHVN Nov 4 2025 (-40%). SESN/ATNX/ICPT still unmeasurable.

queue.question:
Refined FDA CRL sub-hypothesis: test specifically for 'single-asset small biotech with CLINICAL deficiency CRL.'
Criteria: (1) Market cap <$500M at time of CRL, (2) Clinical/efficacy deficiency (not CMC-only), 
(3) Drug represented >50% of company pipeline.
Events to find: SESN-2021, ATNX-2021, NERV-2024, APLT-2024, OTLK-2023, ICPT-2023, plus 8-10 more from 2019-2024.
For each: classify as CMC vs clinical, check single-asset status. Backtest ONLY the clinical+single-asset subset.
HYPOTHESIS TO TEST: SHORT single-asset small biotech on clinical CRL announcement, hold 10 days.
Literature predicts: -8-11% 3-day CAR for clinical CRL vs -3.4% for CMC CRL.
queue.reasoning: Generic CRL short is dead end, but refined clinical+single-asset CRL may still show consistent large negative returns. The 3 cases we measured (OTLK -81%, APLT -77%, VRCA -65%) all had clinical/severe CRL issues. Need N=15 to test.
queue.findings: Partial progress. Found 6 measurable clinical CRL events via FDA CRL API (api.fda.gov/transparency/crl.json). Key individual results from SHORT-at-open strategy: ARDX 2021 (3d=-15.3%, 5d=-28.9%), APLT 2024 (3d=-23.3%, 5d=-42.1%), OTLK 2023 (3d=-19.4%, 5d=-19.8%), BHVN 2025 (3d=+2.1%, recovery), ALDX 2026 (3d=+64.8%, sharp recovery), NERV 2024 (pre-anticipated, +1%). Critical finding: 3 of 6 events with >60% initial crash show consistent 15-40% continued decline over 3-5 days. 2 events with <50% crash show RECOVERY. Need N=8+ to test statistically. Next step: find 3+ more confirmed clinical-primary CRL events with >60% gap-down. Check: SESN Aug 13 2021 (Tiingo has no data), ATNX Mar 1 2021 (Tiingo no data), look for other clinical-primary CRLs from FDA API. Cannot form hypothesis yet - N=3 pure clinical cases insufficient. Task 56d44482 (delisted data) completed separately.

queue.question: Investigate whether the 40% delisted ticker drop rate introduces upward or downward bias. Strategy: identify a sample of delisted tickers from the cluster dataset and manually check Yahoo Finance ticker changes (e.g., acquired companies often trade under parent ticker). This could recover some data.
queue.reasoning: Survivorship bias is the #1 methodological threat to this hypothesis. Quantifying the direction of bias is critical before elevating confidence.
queue.findings: COMPLETED. Sample of 60 failed 2021 cluster tickers classified: 42.9% are acquisitions/mergers (POLY/HP, SIX, SNPO, UNVR, OLO, SYNL, SWM/MATV, SRLP, NCBS), 7.1% bankruptcies/distressed (BTTR, NMTR), 10% SPACs/pre-IPO (NDAC, LEGO, RIVN), 40% unknown. CRITICAL FINDING: Acquisition:Bankruptcy ratio is 6:1. Since acquired stocks receive 20-40% premium, the bias is FAVORABLE (upward). Our measured 3-5% abnormal return UNDERSTATES the true effect. This STRENGTHENS confidence in the hypothesis rather than being a methodological concern. The 40% failure rate does NOT introduce pessimistic bias.

queue.question: Test whether the cluster signal is stronger for high-conviction clusters (n_insiders >= 3) vs minimum clusters (n_insiders == 2). The Alldredge & Blank literature suggests strength increases with cluster size.
queue.reasoning: Subsetting by cluster size may sharpen the signal and reduce noise from borderline cases.
queue.findings: CONFIRMED: 3+ insider clusters show dramatically stronger signal than 2-insider minimum. Discovery 2021 (n=50): 1d avg +3.29%, 72% positive rate, p=0.0012, passes_multiple_testing=True. OOS 2022 (n=66): 1d avg +3.47%, 80% positive rate, p<0.0001; 5d avg +4.85%, 71% positive rate. For comparison, 2-insiders-only 2022: 1d avg +1.88%, 58% positive rate. New hypothesis 4a981cc8 created (confidence=8). 3+ filter reduces 10b5-1 plan contamination. Key finding: signal is regime-robust (72% discovery bull, 80% OOS bear market).

queue.findings_summary: BLCO signal too old (last purchase March 6, 15+ days ago). Window has expired for 1d or 5d hold. WD (Walker & Dunlop) is a better secondary candidate: 3 insiders filed March 20 2026, last purchase March 18 (fresh signal), near 52W low, hypothesis 76678219 assigned.

queue.question: Refined FDA CRL hypothesis: gather 10+ clinical CRL events for single-asset small biotech (2019-2024). Current cases: OTLK-2023 (-81%), APLT-2024 (-77%), VRCA-2022 (-65%). Need 7+ more. Search approach: (1) FDA Complete Response Letters database, (2) BioPharma Catalyst calendar, (3) STAT News FDA decision tracker. Find delisted companies too (use SEC EDGAR to find 8-K filings mentioning CRL). Must classify each as CMC vs clinical deficiency. Do NOT use yfinance for delisted - try Polygon/Tiingo.
queue.reasoning: Clinical CRL for single-asset biotech shows 3 strong cases but N=3 is insufficient. Need N≥10 to test hypothesis.
queue.findings: Superseded by clinical_efficacy_failure_short hypothesis (d302c84b) which combines FDA CRL and Phase 2/3 failures into a single N=11 backtest. The N=10+ clinical CRL target was never reached but the combined hypothesis achieves better statistical power with N=11 and stronger literature support.

queue.question: Build FDA clinical CRL short-from-open hypothesis. Strategy: SHORT small biotech at open the morning after a clinical-efficacy CRL announcement (when initial gap-down is >60%). Measure continued drift over 3-5 days. CURRENT DATA: ARDX Jul 2021 (3d=-15.3%), APLT Nov 2024 (3d=-23.3%), OTLK Aug 2023 (3d=-19.4%). Need 5 more events. Sources to check: (1) FDA CRL API: api.fda.gov/transparency/crl.json - 25 clinical-efficacy CRLs found, check: Disc Medicine (IRON, Feb 2026), SYDNEXIS (Oct 2025), LIB Therapeutics (Dec 2025), Biohaven (BHVN Nov 2025 - already measured, recovery case), (2) SESN Aug 13 2021 - check SEC 8-K for price range before/after, (3) BioPharma Catalyst historical rejections. PRE-REGISTRATION REQUIRED before adding events. Threshold: Only include events where initial gap-down is >60% (avoids contaminating with recovery cases like BHVN -40%). If N>=8 confirms: pre-register hypothesis as SHORT at open, 3d hold, expected return -15% to -25%.
queue.reasoning: FDA clinical CRL clinical-primary post-crash drift: n=3 strong cases show 15-23% continued decline over 3 days from open after >60% crash. This is actionable if confirmed at N>=8. FDA CRL API gives systematic access to find more events. Causal mechanism: (1) institutional risk managers continue exiting over days, (2) retail slow to react, (3) options gamma pressure. IMPORTANT: only clinical-primary CRLs, not CMC (manufacturing). NOT applicable for pre-anticipated CRLs.
queue.findings: Expanded N from 3 to 5 continuation events: ARDX, GRTX (new!), OTLK (Aug 2023), NERV, APLT. All 5 show continuation: avg 3d=-19.83%, avg 5d=-29.34%, 0% positive rate. Passes multiple testing. NEW: GRTX Aug 10 2023 (Galera avasopasem, -82.8% gap, 3d=-21.6%) added as 5th event. REVERSAL pattern fully documented: REPL (diversified pipeline), ALDX 2026 (3rd CRL), SPRY (device/usability CRL), OTLK 2025 (same company 2nd CRL + ultra-micro-cap). BLOCKING: Still need N=7+ for proper 3-validation-instance OOS split. FBRX Sep 2021 is a clinical trial FAILURE (not CRL) - shows same pattern, different category. Phase 2/3 failure continuation queued as new research category.

queue.question: Backtest Phase 2/3 clinical trial failure continuation: SHORT small single-drug biotech at crash-day open after Phase 2/3 primary endpoint failure announcement, hold 3-5 days. Known event: FBRX Sep 3 2021 (FB-401 Phase 2b failure, -80.2% gap, 3d abnormal=-27.8%). Use SEC EDGAR 8-K full-text search for "primary endpoint" + "did not meet" + "failed" to find 10+ events from 2019-2024. Cross-reference with yfinance/Tiingo for >55% crash. This broadens the FDA CRL clinical short into a more frequent event type. Same causal mechanism: (1) revenue projections removed overnight, (2) institutional risk managers force-exit over 3-5 days, (3) retail slow to react.
queue.reasoning: FBRX Sep 2021 shows the Phase 2/3 failure pattern is identical to the FDA CRL continuation pattern (both have >55% crash, single-drug company, clinical endpoint failure). Phase 2/3 failures are MORE FREQUENT than CRLs (many drugs never reach NDA stage). This could expand the N from 5 (CRLs only) to 15+ (CRLs + Phase 2/3 failures) which would enable proper OOS split and hypothesis pre-registration.
queue.findings: Phase 2/3 failure research COMPLETED and MERGED with FDA CRL hypothesis into 'clinical_efficacy_failure_short' (d302c84b). Corrected previous session's wrong event dates: ATHX crash was 2022-05-20 (not 2021-11-30), PRQR crash was 2022-02-11 (not 2022-12-06). Clean Phase 2/3 failure dataset (>55% crash): FBRX 2021-09-03 (-82.3%), PRQR 2022-02-11 (-75.4%), ATHX 2022-05-20 (-65.5%), GOSS 2026-02-23 (-80.1%), IMMP 2026-03-13 (-82.6%). All 5 show continuation (3d abnormal: -26.0%, -24.3%, -41.4%, -37.2%, -26.2%). REVERSAL case: AVIR 2021-10-19 (-66% crash, +12.4% 3d) -- excluded by single-pipeline filter. Combined N=11 (6 CRL + 5 Phase 2/3): avg 3d abnormal=-27.45%, 0% positive rate, p=0.001. Temporal OOS: discovery 2019-2022 avg -28.45%, validation 2023-2026 avg -26.26%. Confidence 8.

queue.question: Do investment-grade-to-junk credit rating downgrades (fallen angels) cause significant negative abnormal stock returns? Check if announcement day still has residual -3%+ effect after pre-announcement trading.
queue.reasoning: Academic literature is clear on negative bond returns but equity effect is less certain. Low priority because we already have clinical efficacy short signal.
queue.findings: DEAD END. Fallen angel credit downgrades (IG-to-junk) not actionable as short equity signal. Issues: (1) events cluster in 2020 COVID crisis making isolation impossible, (2) only 4-5 clean non-crisis events in 2018-2024 (insufficient for OOS split), (3) GE Oct 2018 downgrade day saw +7% (pre-anticipated/relief), (4) academic literature shows bond markets more affected than equity. Dead end recorded in knowledge_base.json.

queue.question: Refined buyback sub-hypothesis: small-cap (<$2B mcap) first-ever buyback programs buying back >10% of float. Do these generate significant short-term abnormal returns? Build EDGAR 8-K scanner to find these events 2019-2024, then backtest.
queue.reasoning: General large buyback signal is dead (market efficient post-2007). But extreme-size first-ever buybacks at small-caps may still signal undervaluation meaningfully. Requires EDGAR 8-K item 8.01 parsing to find events. Academic paper by Vermaelen shows 16% long-horizon excess returns for large tender offers but this has not been tested for small-cap open-market repurchases.

queue.question: Explore reverse merger / blank check SPAC completion announcements: when a SPAC announces merger target (De-SPAC), measure abnormal returns at 1d, 3d, 5d. The initial announcement causes large positive move for target. Key question: does the SPAC acquiring company (SPAC itself) show positive or negative drift after announcement? Academic finding: SPAC acquirers underperform by 15-30% in first year post-merger, but ANNOUNCEMENT day effect may be different.
queue.reasoning: SPAC market produced many events 2020-2022. The mechanism is clear: initial enthusiasm followed by dilution reality. Short the SPAC acquirer 1-5 days after announcement? Or look at the target company optionality.
queue.findings: DEAD END. SPAC De-SPAC announcement day effect: 5 events measured, 1d pos_rate=40%, avg=-0.38% (p=0.92). Direction heterogeneous. Problems: (1) Leak contamination - CCIV pre-ran +400% before official announcement; (2) Regime-specific to 2020-2021 SPAC bubble (post-Q1 2021 market normalized to $10); (3) 3 of 8 events delisted/unmeasurable. Dead end recorded in knowledge base.

queue.question: For Phase 3 FDA approvals/rejections of novel drugs (not generics, not supplements), what is the typical abnormal return at 1d and 5d horizons? Does the effect differ between first-time submissions and resubmissions after a Complete Response Letter?
queue.reasoning: FDA decisions are binary events with clear timing and a direct causal channel (revenue expectations). High survivorship bias risk makes this a good stress test for our bias protocols. Resubmission vs first-time is an unexplored sub-question.
queue.findings: FDA PDUFA literature review completed. Key finding: effect is SIZE-DEPENDENT. Large pharma: ~+0.4% approval return (NOT actionable). Small biotech: +8-11% abnormal vs pharma. Rejection (CRL): -4.3% to -9.2% 3-day CAR, asymmetrically negative. Approval rate at PDUFA: ~75-85%. Post-rejection drift: continues for 2+ weeks. CRL deficiency type matters (CMC vs clinical). DEFERRED: Full backtest of small biotech FDA approvals/rejections needed for next session. Must include failed/delisted companies to address survivorship bias. Most promising pattern: SHORT after CRL rejection, small biotech.

queue.question: Test PEAD for smaller S&P 500 components (market cap $5B-$30B) with >10% EPS beats. Classic PEAD literature finds the effect concentrated in smaller, less-covered stocks. Find 20+ S&P 500 mid-cap earnings beats from 2020-2024, measure abnormal returns at 1d, 5d, 10d, 20d. Distinct from mega-cap test (which showed no PEAD). Check analyst coverage as moderator: fewer analysts = stronger drift?
queue.reasoning: Our mega-cap PEAD test showed NO drift (discovery: +1.05% 1d, negative drift after). Literature says PEAD is concentrated in small/mid-caps with fewer analysts. Testing mid-cap S&P 500 components addresses the academic prediction. This is a genuine test of whether information asymmetry drives PEAD.
queue.findings: Dead end. N=19 mid-cap events (WING/TREX/IBP/OLLI/FIVE): 1d avg=+0.01%, 20d avg=+1.27%, all p-values 0.67-0.99, 50% positive rate at 20d = random. Confirms PEAD is primarily a microcap phenomenon. Not actionable.

queue.question:
Explore dividend capture strategy: what is the abnormal return in the 3-5 days before an ex-dividend date
for high-yield dividend stocks? Does the price run up (dividend capture buying) and then fall (on ex-dividend date + 1)?
Test S&P 500 stocks with dividend yield >3%. Find 15+ ex-dividend dates from 2021-2024. Key question: is there systematic 
abnormal return of >1.5% in the pre-ex-dividend period, separate from the mechanical dividend drop on ex-date?
Also: what is the post-ex-dividend drift for stocks that cut dividends (compare to increases)?
queue.reasoning: Dividend capture is well-documented in literature with clear causal mechanism (institutional window-dressing around dividend dates). Complementary to index addition work. No survivorship bias issues for dividend payers.
queue.findings: Dead end. Pre-ex-dividend run-up for high-yield stocks (VZ/MO/T/PM/KMI): avg 3d abnormal +0.60% (discovery) vs +0.21% (validation). Below 1.5% threshold. No multiple testing pass. Effect is ~0.4% average - too small to trade.

queue.question: VWAP breakout momentum: does expanding to 100+ stock universe and filtering for calm VIX regime validate a hypothesis-quality signal? Test thresholds 20% and 25% above VWAP. Net return after costs needs to clear 1% minimum.
queue.reasoning: Discovered as opposite of tested disposition effect. 489 events over 20 stocks shows +0.64-1.19% abnormal returns, all horizons, OOS confirmed. High-volume variant is strongest. But economic edge after costs is thin (~0.42-0.82% net). Need larger universe, tighter threshold, and regime filter before a hypothesis can be formed. Tools exist: tools/vwap_deviation_signal.py

queue.question: Wells Notice sub-hypothesis: Are financial fraud Wells Notices different from procedural ones? If yes, test 10+ fraud-specific Wells Notices (like BEN -13%, NRDS -7%) vs procedural (NDAQ +1.5%, XYL -2.5%). Need manual classification or a legal database. Likely requires Bloomberg Law or SEC enforcement actions database. LOW PRIORITY: would need external data source.
queue.reasoning: Wells Notice full sample failed multiple testing but directional pattern exists at 1d (68% negative). Fraud vs procedural is the key discriminator. Cannot test without case classification.

queue.question: Activist 13D first-news-date signal: Test activist campaigns using the FIRST NEWS COVERAGE DATE (not SEC filing date). The 10-day filing window means prices move on news leaks, not official filings. Would need Bloomberg News/Refinitiv to get publication dates. Brav et al (2008) found +7% for 2001-2006 using filing dates - this has decayed. But the causal mechanism is still valid. Test if usable via press release scraping (company press releases when activist publicly announces position). MEDIUM PRIORITY.
queue.reasoning: Activist 13D filing date signal fully decayed in 2020-2024 (avg +0.26% 1d, N=24 Starboard events). However academic support for the phenomenon is strong. Signal may be accessible via press release date rather than SEC filing date.

queue.question: Build EDGAR monitoring tool: daily scan of biotech 8-K filings for primary endpoint failure language. Use EDGAR EFTS full-text search API. Should flag: company name, ticker, date, keywords found. Run this at start of each session to catch qualifying events for d302c84b.
queue.reasoning: The current approach of manually searching for qualifying events is slow and reactive. An automated EDGAR scanner would catch qualifying events within hours of the 8-K filing, enabling same-day trade setup.
queue.findings: COMPLETED: tools/edgar_clinical_failure_scanner.py was already built in Session 6. The tool scans EDGAR EFTS full-text search for 8-K filings with clinical failure language in last N days. Validated this session - run at start of each session to check for qualifying d302c84b events. Added to session startup routine.

queue.question: Is VWAP_252 breakout (Close/VWAP >= 1.15) a viable LONG momentum signal? Can it be traded profitably?
queue.reasoning: VWAP deviation backtest revealed the OPPOSITE of the disposition effect: stocks extended above VWAP continue upward. Signal has n=489, passes_multiple_testing=True, +0.64% abnormal at 1d, all bootstrap CIs exclude zero, OOS 2024 confirms (+0.82% 1d). Need regime analysis, higher-threshold test, and transaction cost viability check before forming hypothesis.
